element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player selects first opponent freely, establishing baseline combat mechanics
Veteran character with established moveset, moderate difficulty
Veteran character with established moveset, moderate difficulty
New mechanic allowing attack chaining; brief invincibility window on successful hits enables execution
Pressing Light Punch/Light Kick on opposite line requires spatial awareness and timing
Ability to jump between lines; noted as occasionally annoying in execution
Former CPU-exclusive character; increased difficulty tier
Former CPU-exclusive character; increased difficulty tier
Former CPU-exclusive character; increased difficulty tier
Returning opponent with mastered mechanics; significant difficulty spike
Series staple final boss; maximum difficulty

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Conditional: after winning every match in two rounds
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Hidden dream match; represents mastery condition achievement; cross-series opponent

typical_relief_triggers: Defeating first 8 standard characters (player-selected order reduces pressure)
Victory with two-round sweep (unlocks dream match progression)
typical_tension_triggers: Transition to boss rush (Billy, Axel, Laurence, Geese, Krauser)
Faster game speed compared to Fatal Fury 2
Two-round sweep requirement for secret content
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Front-loaded difficulty ramp with player-controlled early tension management, accelerating into deterministic boss gauntlet; speed increase from predecessor maintains momentum

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Winning every match in two rounds triggers Ryo Sakazaki dream match
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Conditional boss appearance based on win condition mastery

difficulty_curve.early_game: Permissive: player selects first opponent from roster; 8 standard characters provide baseline difficulty ceiling
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating: transition to former CPU-exclusive bosses (Billy, Axel, Laurence) introduces new difficulty tier
difficulty_curve.late_game: Punishing: Geese Howard and Wolfgang Krauser represent series-defining difficulty peaks; speed increase from FFS2 maintains pressure
notable_spikes: Boss rush begins after 10 matches (formerly AI-only characters now required)
Ryo Sakazaki encounter (conditional on perfect execution)

notable_level_patterns.description: Single-player mode allows player to choose first opponent from 8 standard characters, establishing player agency before scripted boss progression. First 10 matches can be completed in any order against roster.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tournament start - all 8 standard characters available

notable_level_patterns.description: After 10 opponent matches, player faces mandatory sequence: Billy → Axel → Laurence → Geese → Krauser. No opponent selection; deterministic difficulty escalation.
Ryo Sakazaki appears as hidden final boss only if player wins every match in exactly two rounds. Mastery-gated secret content; requires flawless execution across entire tournament.
